name: Butter-Rich Roll & Bread Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Select the Dough setting.
2. When complete, remove dough. Choose shaping method.
3. Cover dough and let stand for 15 minutes before baking.
4. Bake as directed in each shaping variation until golden brown.
Egg Glaze:
1. Beat together egg and water. Brush generously over shaped loaves or rolls prior to baking.
Traditional Loaf:
1. On a lightly-floured surface, divide dough into two halves.
2. Shape each half into loaf shape.
3. Place in 2 greased 8x4 inch loaf pans.
4. Brush with egg glaze if desired.
5. Cover dough and let stand for 15 minutes before baking at 375°F (190°C) for 25-35 minutes. Makes 2 loaves.
Pan Rolls:
1. On a lightly-floured surface, shape dough into a ball.
2. Divide dough into equal quarters.
3. Cut each quarter into 6 pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
4. Place each ball in muffin cups.
5. Brush with egg glaze if desired.
6. Cover dough and let stand for 15 minutes before baking at 375°F (190°C) for 25-35 minutes. Makes 24 rolls.
Cloverleaf Rolls:
1. On a lightly-floured surface, shape dough into a ball.
2. Divide into quarters.
3. Divide each quarter into 6 pieces.
4. Cut each piece into 3 sections and shape each into a ball.
5. Place 3 balls together in each muffin cup.
6. Brush with egg glaze if desired.
7. Cover dough and let stand for 15 minutes before baking at 375°F (190°C) for 25-35 minutes. Makes 24 rolls.
Cinnamon-Raisin Rolls:
1. Turn dough out onto a lightly-floured board.
2. Roll into a 12x14 inch rectangle.
3. Spread surface with 1/3 cup butter, thinly sliced.
4. Sprinkle surface with 1/3 cup brown sugar and 1 1/2 teaspoons cinnamon.
5. Sprinkle 1/2 cup raisins over dough.
6. Start with the 12-inch side, roll dough in a jelly-roll style, pinch seam to seal.
7. With the seam side down, cut into (12) 1-inch pieces.
8. Place on a greased baking sheet, 2 inches apart.
9. Cover lightly with a damp towel and allow to rise in a warm place until doubled, about 40 minutes.
10. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 10-15 minutes. Makes 12 rolls.
Cinnamon-Raisin Swirl Loaf:
1. On a lightly-floured surface, roll half of the dough into a 12x8 inch rectangle.
2. Melt 3 tablespoons butter and brush over dough.
3. Sprinkle dough with a mix of 1/3 cup sugar and 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cinnamon.
4. Sprinkle 1/2 cup raisins over dough.
5. Beginning with the long side of dough, roll up in a jelly-roll style.
6. Seal ends by pinching edges of dough together with fingertips.
7. Place dough in a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan or on a large baking sheet.
8. Allow to stand for 15 minutes before baking at 375°F (190°C) for 30-40 minutes until golden brown. Glaze with mix of 1 cup sifted powdered sugar and 1 1/2 tablespoons milk when cool. Makes 2 loaves.
Bread Sticks:
1. Turn dough out onto a lightly-floured board and shape into a ball.
2. Divide into 24 equal pieces for 16-inch sticks.
3. Roll each piece of dough into thin ropes, about 16 inches long.
4. Arrange ropes about 1 inch apart on an oiled baking sheet.
5. Cover with egg glaze if desired.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 10-15 minutes. Makes 24 breadsticks.
